capabilities: Combine Event Sourcing with AI-prompted knowledge bases to maintain an immutable event log as the source of truth, ensuring data integrity and traceability.

procedure.action: Capture event specifications using domain-appropriate tooling by ensuring each event is serialized to JSON and stored in an append-only log; verify schema compliance with JSON Schema draft-07.
Assert that error_count < 1 and result_valid = true for each captured event by running automated validation scripts; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to an audit trail for traceability.

Log operation_duration_ms and result_code to an audit trail by appending structured log entries to a centralized logging system; ensure log entries include timestamp, event_id, and user_id using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Assert event specifications using domain-appropriate tooling by cross-referencing event data against predefined schemas; ensure all fields pass validation checks before processing by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.
Inject criteria and metrics using domain-appropriate tooling by defining key performance indicators (KPIs) such as event_processing_time_p95 < 200ms; monitor these metrics continuously.

summary: Evaluate the integration of Event Sourcing with AI-prompted knowledge bases by defining success metrics, criteria, and validation steps to ensure data integrity and performance.
title: Event Sourcing

failureConditions: Data inconsistency occurs when event logs are not immutable, causing discrepancies in derived knowledge bases.
Performance degradation when event processing time exceeds thresholds, leading to delayed knowledge base updates.
Schema validation failures when event data does not conform to predefined schemas, causing processing errors.
metrics: that error_count < 1 and result_valid = true for each captured event by running automated validation scripts
event specifications using domain-appropriate tooling by cross-referencing event data against predefined schemas
